content:
1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning. Useful for a second opinion or a paper-style sketch.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Dispatch synchronously** (not via background `Bash` with `run_in_background: true`). Background dispatch leaves the parent's session log stuck on "running in background" on the dashboard.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | — | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and clears the file after the plan phase succeeds. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/` (`prover-autoformalize.md` / `prover-prover.md` / `prover-polish.md`).
127	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
128	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
129	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
130	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
131	
132	### Subagents
133	
134	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
135	
136	### Review Agent
137	
138	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
139	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
140	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
141	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
142	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
143	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
144	
145	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
146	
147	These run automatically between phases:
148	
149	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
150	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
151	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
152

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs in your assigned `.lean` file.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas on your file.
9	3. Check the `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments (file-specific user hints).
10	4. **Read the relevant blueprint chapter before writing Lean code.** The chapter holds the mathematical proof sketch you must align with. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs. Push as far as possible.
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you can't fully close a sorry, leave your best attempt — commented-out steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` if needed), but your work must be visible for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to a bare `sorry` — that wastes the work.
13	7. Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
14	
15	**Write permissions**: only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	`archon-protected.yaml` lists declarations with **frozen signatures**. You may fill their proof bodies but must not rename, re-type, reorder arguments, or weaken hypotheses. Only the mathematician edits protected signatures.
20	
21	## Avoid early termination
22	
23	- Don't abandon a proof prematurely. Many complex proofs run to thousands of lines.
24	- Difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave a `sorry`.
25	- Don't delegate to "the next iteration" or "another prover" if more effort could close it.
26	- Only modify the proof for your assigned task — leave unrelated proofs untouched.
27	- **Decompose**: break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure when available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each individually until the entire goal closes.
28	
29	## Task completion criteria
30	
31	Your task is complete ONLY when ALL of:
32	
33	1. Every `sorry` in scope is replaced with a complete proof.
34	2. Zero axioms introduced.
35	3. The file compiles cleanly.
36	
37	## Never weaken the type to dodge the proof
38	
39	When the substantive type is unattainable this iter, you have one honest move: leave `sorry` with the **intended type signature** so the type itself encodes the claim. You must NOT substitute a tautologically-true type to trivialize the body. Three patterns are banned:
40	
41	- **Reflexive-iso placeholder.** Replacing a non-trivial `X ≅ Y` (or `Nonempty (X ≅ Y)`) target with `Nonempty (X ≅ X) := ⟨Iso.refl _⟩`. The type now asserts nothing; the docstring's "iter-N placeholder" disclosure does not undo the structural lie, and the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference will resolve to this hollow declaration.
42	- **`Classical.choice` body around an explicit witness.** Writing `Classical.choice (α := X) ⟨witness⟩` to dissolve `Type` vs `Prop` friction — the witness is unreachable through any unfold, so the def is indistinguishable from `Classical.choice ⟨any-other-X⟩` downstream. Either use `Classical.choose` on the existential chain (preserves the spec), or `Nonempty.some`-with-witness; or ship a typed `sorry`.
43	- **Empty-content `proof_wanted`.** `proof_wanted` discards the declaration post-elaboration — the named decl never enters the environment, so the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference breaks. Use `theorem name : T := by sorry` with the intended signature instead.
44	
45	**Litmus test**: if you `unfold` your declaration, does it expose the named substantive content (Kähler differential module, the explicit iso, …) or does it stop at `Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / nothing? If the latter, the body is structurally vacuous — ship the typed `sorry` instead.
46	
47	## When infrastructure is missing
48	
49	Do NOT report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up:
50	
51	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`): "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
52	2. **Try the alternative** — formalize whatever the informal agent suggests.
53	3. **If you still can't**: write the alternative sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md` and record in your task result what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found. "I couldn't prove X, but here's approach Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than "infrastructure missing".
54	
55	When stuck more generally: break into smaller subgoals, search Mathlib more thoroughly, prove missing helpers yourself, try alternative strategies, re-read the blueprint, use Web Search for published proofs.
56	
57	**Impossibility vs difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
58	
59	## Proof style
60	
61	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its body.
62	- Keep edits minimal; don't delete comments or change labels; don't add unrelated declarations.
63	- Helper lemmas you introduced may be modified if they turn out wrong.
64	- Add a concise comment above each helper lemma so reuse is easy.
65	- **`change` vs `show`** — `change` reshapes the goal up to defeq; `show` is purely display-level annotation. Using `show` where `change` is needed produces a linter warning. Default to `change` when in doubt.
66	
67	## Mathlib tags in PROGRESS.md
68	
69	The plan agent tags suggested lemmas:
70	
71	- `[verified]` — confirmed to exist.
72	- `[expected]` — guessed by naming convention. Quick `lean_local_search`; pivot if it doesn't exist.
73	- `[gap]` — verified NOT in Mathlib. Don't waste search time; formalize a workaround.
74	
75	## LSP MCP tools
76	
77	The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls** (full names like `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal` / `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`). The lean4 skill reference uses short names (`lean_goal`, `lean_local_search`, …) — same tools, NOT shell binaries.
78	
79	- Always invoke through your tool-call interface, the same way as `Read` or `Grep`.
80	- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` etc. — there is no such shell command.
81	- First LSP action: `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your file. If it returns `success: false` the server is cold; retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.
82	
83	## Search protocol
84	
85	Follow `references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`. Key priority:
86	
87	1. `lean_local_search` first.
88	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — describe the mathematical content, not just the name.
89	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only.
90	4. Never use shell `find` / `grep` to locate Mathlib theorems.
91	
92	## Tooling traps
93	
94	- **Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics**: in `lean_multi_attempt`, `diagnostics: []` can be misleading (line-scoping quirks attach errors to the enclosing `by` block). Check that `goals_after` is empty or has advanced.
95	- **Beware `lean_run_code` with imports**: standalone snippets can silently swallow elaboration errors when they contain imports. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
96	
97	## Logging
98	
99	Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror your `.lean` path: `Algebra/WLocal.lean` → `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`):
100	
101	```markdown
102	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
103	
104	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
105	### Attempt 1
106	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
107	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance, not available
108	- **Dead end:** direct case split without IsLocalRing
109	
110	### Attempt 2
111	- **Approach:** Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
112	- **Result:** RESOLVED
113	- **Key insight:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective` bridges the gap
114	
115	## helper_bijective (line 78)
116	### Attempt 1
117	- **Approach:** Split into injectivity + surjectivity; prove injectivity
118	- **Result:** PARTIAL (branch closed) — injectivity closed; surjectivity remains
119	- **Next step:** `PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective` for the right branch
120	- **Lemmas found:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective`
121	```
122	
123	**Rules**: one section per theorem/lemma in your file. Each attempt records approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps. Log negative search results ("Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib"). The plan agent merges these into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md` next iter.
124	
125	**Read-only context**: read `task_pending.md` (prior attempts on your file) and `task_done.md` (when the problem resembles a completed one). Do not write to either.
126	
127	## End-of-session handoff
128	
129	Before stopping:
130	
131	1. Write `task_results/<your_file>.md` with: current result, lemmas discovered, concrete next step, dead-end warnings.
132	2. Save all changes; ensure the file compiles (use scoped `sorry` for stuck parts if needed).
133

65	## Algebra structure on `Γ(X, O_X)`
66	
67	The global-section ring `Γ(X, O_X)` for `X` over `Spec k` is a
68	`k`-algebra via the structure-morphism composition `k → Γ(Spec k, ⊤) →
69	Γ(X, ⊤)`. We expose this as `AlgebraicGeometry.gammaAlgebra` so the
70	S3 lemmas and their consumer in `ChartAlgebra.lean` share a single
71	algebra-instance definition. The consumer's local definition
72	
73	```
74	set α : (CommRingCat.of k) ⟶ X.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤) :=
75	  (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).inv ≫ (X ↘ Spec (CommRingCat.of k)).appTop
76	letI algkΓ : Algebra k ↥(X.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤)) := α.hom.toAlgebra
77	```
78	
79	equals `gammaAlgebra k X` definitionally.
80	-/
81	
82	open CategoryTheory Limits
83	
84	universe u
85	
86	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
87	
88	/-- The canonical `k → Γ(X, O_X)` ring map for `X` over `Spec k`: precompose
89	the inverse of `Scheme.ΓSpecIso` with the `appTop` of the structure morphism.
90	This is the algebra map underpinning `gammaAlgebra k X`. -/
91	noncomputable def gammaAlgebraMap (k : Type u) [CommRing k] (X : Scheme.{u})
92	    [X.Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))] :
93	    (CommRingCat.of k) ⟶ X.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤) :=
94	  (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).inv ≫ (X ↘ Spec (CommRingCat.of k)).appTop
95	
96	/-- The canonical `Algebra k Γ(X, O_X)` structure on global sections of `X` over
97	`Spec k`. Matches the local `algkΓ` definition used by the consumer in
98	`AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/ChartAlgebra.lean` (`constants_integral_over_base_field`). -/
99	@[reducible]
100	noncomputable def gammaAlgebra (k : Type u) [CommRing k] (X : Scheme.{u})
101	    [X.Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))] :
102	    Algebra k ↥(X.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤)) :=
103	  (gammaAlgebraMap k X).hom.toAlgebra
104	
105	-- Mathlib's `Algebra.TensorProduct` ships only the LEFT algebra instance on
106	-- `B₁ ⊗[k] B₂`; the symmetric right-algebra is a `local instance` inside
107	-- `Mathlib.RingTheory.IsTensorProduct` (also re-enabled in `ChartAlgebra.lean`).
108	-- Re-enable it here so `Algebra K (TensorProduct k Γ K)` resolves for the
109	-- target type of `Gamma_baseChange_iso_tensor_of_proper`.
110	attribute [local instance] Algebra.TensorProduct.rightAlgebra
111	
112	/-- **(S3.sep.1)** Smooth proper schemes over a field have geometrically reduced
113	global sections.
114	
115	For `X` smooth proper over a field `k`, the global-section `k`-algebra
116	`Γ(X, O_X)` is geometrically reduced: `Algebra.IsGeometricallyReduced k
117	↥(X.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤))`.
118	
119	Blueprint: `lem:S3_sep_1_smooth_geometrically_reduced_Gamma`
120	(`blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex`), with closure path
121	Stacks 0334 + 04QM (smooth ⇒ formally smooth ⇒ geometrically reduced
122	fibres) and consumption of (S3.pi.1) `Gamma_baseChange_iso_tensor_of_proper`.
123

183	`Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap k K))`. The `Algebra k Γ`
184	structure on the source is `gammaAlgebra k X`; the `Algebra K _` on the

205	      ⟨pullback.snd _ _⟩

1	# Analogy: H1Cotangent / Γ-vanishing pivot for rigidity_over_kbar (M2.a closure)
2

4

11	## Iteration
12	
13	150
14	
15	## Structural problem (abstracted)
16	
17	We have a smooth proper geometrically irreducible scheme `X` over a field `k` and need to conclude that a chart-algebra section `b : B` annihilated by the universal Kähler derivation `D : B → Ω_{B/k}` lies in the image of `algebraMap k B`. The currently-committed path (b) decomposes this into a global cohomology statement `Γ(X, O_X) ≅ k` (sub-claims (S3.sep.1), (S3.sep.2), (S3.pi.1), (S3.pi.2)). The directive asks whether (a) the SAME content is available in Mathlib via the typeclass `Algebra.H1Cotangent k B`, (b) `rigidity_over_kbar` could consume `Subsingleton (H1Cotangent k B)` instead of `Γ ≅ k`, (c) alternative shapes (formal-scheme-style, Hopf-algebra-style, joint-derivation-kernel-style) exist elsewhere in Mathlib that could be ported.
18	
19	The underlying mathematical pattern is "structure-equation cohomology in degree 0 = the base ring", encompassing both global-sections statements (Γ(X, O_X) = k for proper integral / connected) and chart-algebra statements (joint kernel of coordinate derivations = k in standard-smooth chart).
20	
21	## Failed approaches (from directive)
22	
23	- **Path (a) BUILD proper-Γ-flat-base-change in-tree** (~250–300 LOC): same Mathlib gap as path (b) step (e); requires Čech-equaliser + flat-tensor-exactness chase. Mathlib `b80f227` ships no `AlgebraicGeometry.IsBaseChange` namespace nor `R^iπ_*` for proper π.
24	- **Path (b) SMART PROOF via four (S3.*) sub-claims** (~310–550 LOC): (S3.pi.1) is the same Mathlib gap as path (a) step (e). Iter-149 status: 0/4 bodies closed.
25	- **KDM (p2) char-0 bridge** (iter-149 (BR.1)–(BR.4) scaffolded, (BR.5) joint-kernel-collapse remains structured sorry): the underlying obstacle is that Mathlib's `Differential.ContainConstants` typeclass packages a SINGLE-derivation property `b' = 0 ⇒ b ∈ range`, not the INTERSECTION of multiple coordinate derivations. Single-derivation kernel is strictly larger than `range (algebraMap k B)`.
26	
27	## Analogues found
28	
29	### Analogue: `Algebra.FormallyUnramified.range_eq_top_of_isPurelyInseparable` + `PerfectField.ofCharZero` + `Algebra.IsAlgebraic.isSeparable_of_perfectField` — CharZero-only collapse of the (S3.sep.*) lane
30	
31	- **Domain**: ring theory + field theory (`Mathlib.RingTheory.Unramified.Field`,
32	  `Mathlib.FieldTheory.Perfect`).
33	- **Same structural problem there**: For a finite field extension `Γ/k` with the
34	  combination "purely inseparable + separable", `algebraMap k Γ` is surjective.
35	  Distance from project's problem: IDENTICAL on the ring side — this is literally
36	  the closing lemma already invoked at `ChartAlgebra.lean:460`
37	  (`IsPurelyInseparable.surjective_algebraMap_of_isSeparable`). The novelty
38	  surfaced here is the CharZero-specific instance chain that closes (b.1)
39	  separability for free.
40	- **Citations (verified live via `lean_leansearch`)**:
41	  - `Algebra.FormallyUnramified.range_eq_top_of_isPurelyInseparable`
42	    (`Mathlib.RingTheory.Unramified.Field`):
43	    `[Field K] [Field L] [Algebra K L] [Algebra.FormallyUnramified K L]
44	     [Algebra.EssFiniteType K L] [IsPurelyInseparable K L] →
45	     (algebraMap K L).range = ⊤`.
46	  - `Algebra.IsAlgebraic.isSeparable_of_perfectField` (instance in
47	    `Mathlib.FieldTheory.Perfect`):
48	    `[Field K] [Field L] [Algebra K L] [Algebra.IsAlgebraic K L] [PerfectField K] →
49	     Algebra.IsSeparable K L`.
50	  - `PerfectField.ofCharZero` (instance in `Mathlib.FieldTheory.Perfect`):
51	    `[Field K] [CharZero K] → PerfectField K`.
52	  - `Algebra.FormallyUnramified.iff_isSeparable`
53	    (`Mathlib.RingTheory.Unramified.Field`):
54	    `[Field K] [Field L] [Algebra K L] [Algebra.EssFiniteType K L] →
55	     (Algebra.FormallyUnramified K L ↔ Algebra.IsSeparable K L)`.
56	  - `Algebra.FormallySmooth.of_perfectField` (instance in
57	    `Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.Field`):
58	    `[Field K] [Field L] [Algebra K L] [PerfectField K] [Algebra.EssFiniteType K L]
59	     → Algebra.FormallySmooth K L`.
60	- **Technique**: In `CharZero`, `PerfectField k` is automatic; every algebraic
61	  extension of a perfect field is separable; so any finite-dimensional `Γ/k`
62	  satisfies `Algebra.IsSeparable k Γ` via the instance chain
63	  `CharZero → PerfectField → IsAlgebraic.isSeparable_of_perfectField`. The
64	  separability lane (b.1) becomes a one-liner. (S3.sep.1) +(S3.sep.2) collapse
65	  into a single `inferInstance` call once `[CharZero k]` is propagated.
66	- **Mapping to project**: The downstream consumers of
67	  `constants_integral_over_base_field` already commit to `[CharZero k]`:
68	  - `KaehlerDifferential.mem_range_algebraMap_of_D_eq_zero`
69	    (`ChartAlgebra.lean:123–194`) has `[CharZero k]`.
70	  - `df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart`
71	    (`ChartAlgebra.lean:222–240`) has `[CharZero k]`.
72

146	### Analogue: `MvPolynomial.mkDerivation` + `MvPolynomial.derivation_C` + `MvPolynomial.derivation_eq_of_forall_mem_vars` — joint-coordinate-derivation kernel directly (KDM (BR.5) shortcut)
147	
148	- **Domain**: commutative algebra / polynomial algebra
149	  (`Mathlib.Algebra.MvPolynomial.Derivation`).
150	- **Same structural problem there**: in `MvPolynomial σ k`, the joint kernel
151	  of all coordinate `pderiv_i` equals `MvPolynomial.C.range` (constants). This
152	  is the FREE case of the joint-kernel-collapse problem.
153	- **Citations (verified)**:
154	  - `MvPolynomial.derivation_C`: `D (C a) = 0` for any derivation `D : Derivation R (MvPolynomial σ R) A`.
155	  - `MvPolynomial.derivation_eq_of_forall_mem_vars`: a derivation is
156	    determined by its values on `f.vars`, i.e. on coordinate-indeterminates
157	    relevant to `f`.
158	  - `MvPolynomial.pderiv` + `MvPolynomial.pderiv_C`: coordinate derivatives
159	    annihilate constants.
160	- **Technique**: For `MvPolynomial σ k` of finite `σ` over a CharZero `k`, the
161	  joint kernel `⋂_i ker(pderiv_i)` equals `MvPolynomial.C.range` by direct
162	  monomial expansion — each monomial `c · ∏ x_i^{e_i}` with any `e_j > 0`
163	  gets sent to `c · e_j · x_j^{e_j - 1} · ∏_{i ≠ j} x_i^{e_i} ≠ 0` by
164	  `pderiv_j` (using `CharZero` to ensure `e_j ≠ 0` doesn't get killed).
165	  Mathlib's `MvPolynomial` API supports this directly via the monomial
166	  basis lemmas.
167	- **Mapping to project**: The KDM (BR.5) joint-kernel-collapse for a STANDARD
168	  SMOOTH chart `B = MvPolynomial σ k / I` reduces to two sub-pieces:
169	  - **Free case** for `MvPolynomial σ k`: handled by direct
170	    `MvPolynomial.pderiv`-monomial argument (~25 LOC).
171	  - **Quotient transfer**: lift the joint-kernel-equals-constants statement
172	    from `MvPolynomial σ k` to `MvPolynomial σ k / I` via the surjection
173	    `MvPolynomial σ k →ₐ[k] B`. For a STANDARD-smooth chart, the surjection
174	    is the kernel-of-Jacobian-invertible map; the kernel `I` contains no
175	    elements of `range C` other than `0`, so the joint-kernel-equals-constants
176	    statement transfers cleanly (~30–40 LOC).
177	- **Porting cost**: **MEDIUM** — ~60–80 LOC total (the directive's
178	  iter-149 estimate of 40–80 LOC for (BR.5) is in range with the lower
179	  half of this estimate). The Mathlib base is ready; no new infrastructure
180	  is needed beyond the quotient-transfer step.
181	- **Verdict**: **ANALOGUE_FOUND**.

183	### Analogue: `Algebra.FormallySmooth.of_perfectField` (chart-side FormallySmooth-for-free in CharZero)
184	
185	- **Domain**: ring theory (`Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.Field`).
186	- **Same structural problem there**: capture "smoothness is automatic" over
187	  perfect fields for essentially-finite-type algebras.
188	- **Citations (verified)**:
189	  - `Algebra.FormallySmooth.of_perfectField` (instance): `[PerfectField K]
190	    [Algebra.EssFiniteType K L] → Algebra.FormallySmooth K L` (L is a field).
191	- **Technique**: derive `FormallySmooth k L` for any essentially-finite-type
192	  L over a perfect k.
193	- **Mapping to project**: For the KDM lemma, the chart algebra `B` is NOT a
194	  field but a chart-ring of positive Krull dimension; `Algebra.FormallySmooth.
195	  of_perfectField` does NOT directly apply. The lemma `Algebra.Smooth.formallySmooth`
196	  combined with `Algebra.instSmoothOfIsStandardSmooth` already supplies
197	  `FormallySmooth k B` for free under the iter-149 `[IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension
198	  n k B]` hypothesis. So FormallySmooth for the chart-side is ALREADY a "free"
199	  premise; the question is what to DO with it.
200	  Having `FormallySmooth k B` gives:
201	  - `Subsingleton (H1Cotangent k B)` via `subsingleton_h1Cotangent`.
202	  - `Module.Projective B Ω[B⁄k]` (already known via `IsStandardSmooth.free_kaehlerDifferential`).
203	  Neither directly closes the KDM kernel-is-constants problem.
204	- **Porting cost**: zero (instance already fires under iter-149 typeclass
205	  setup) — but the benefit to KDM (BR.5) closure is also approximately zero.
206	- **Verdict**: **PARTIAL_ANALOGUE** — clarifies that `FormallySmooth` is
207	  not the right shape for the kernel-of-D problem; H1Cotangent vanishing
208	  is independent content from the joint-derivation-kernel collapse.
209	
210	### Analogue (DISCARDED): `Subsingleton (Algebra.H1Cotangent k B)` as the H1Cotangent-vanishing PIVOT for rigidity_over_kbar
211	
212	- **Citations**: `Algebra.FormallySmooth.subsingleton_h1Cotangent`
213	  (`Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.Basic`),
214	  `Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.subsingleton_h1Cotangent`
215	  (`Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.StandardSmoothCotangent`).
216	- **Reason for discard**: the directive's question (b) "can the chart-algebra
217	  closure chain consume `Subsingleton (Algebra.H1Cotangent k B)` directly,
218	  without routing through the `Γ ≅ k` global statement?" is based on a
219	  SUPERFICIAL similarity that does not survive mathematical scrutiny:
220	  - `Subsingleton (H1Cotangent k B)` is a statement about the SECOND cotangent
221	    cohomology — it says certain extensions `0 → I → R → B → 0` (with
222	    `I^2 = 0`) admit liftings. It is a STRUCTURAL property of the
223	    presentation-of-B, NOT a statement about kernels of derivations.
224	  - `Γ(X, O_X) ≅ k` is a statement about ZEROTH structure-sheaf cohomology
225	    — it says the global sections of `O_X` are exhausted by the base field.
226	  These are independent vanishings. There is no Mathlib theorem that
227	  converts H1Cotangent-vanishing of a chart-algebra into a global Γ ≅ k
228	  statement, nor is there a mathematical bridge: a standard-smooth chart
229	  algebra `B` with `Subsingleton (H1Cotangent k B)` is generic in the sense
230	  that it carries no information about the GLOBAL geometry of any scheme it
231	  glues into. The "cotangent-vanishing" naming collision between Mathlib's
232	  `H1Cotangent` (André-Quillen cohomology) and the project's
233	  "cotangent-vanishing pile" (Γ ≅ k for smooth proper geom-irr X/k)
234	  is exactly that — a naming collision.
235	- The H1Cotangent pivot is **mathematically incoherent** as proposed.
236	- **Verdict**: **NO_USEFUL_ANALOGUE**.
237

125	    {B : Type u} [CommRing B] [Algebra k B] [Algebra.FiniteType k B]
126	    {n : ℕ} [Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n k B]
127	    {b : B} (hDb : _root_.KaehlerDifferential.D k B b = 0) :
128	    b ∈ (algebraMap k B).range := by
129	  -- Reverse inclusion (recorded for downstream symmetry):
130	  -- `D : Derivation k B Ω[B⁄k]` vanishes on the algebraMap image by
131	  -- `Derivation.map_algebraMap`.
132	  have _hRev : ∀ a : k, _root_.KaehlerDifferential.D k B (algebraMap k B a) = 0 :=
133	    fun a => (_root_.KaehlerDifferential.D k B).map_algebraMap a
134	  -- (BR.2) Free-module structure on `Ω[B⁄k]` from the standard-smooth
135	  -- chart hypothesis: `Module.Free B Ω[B⁄k]` of rank `n`. The relative-
136	  -- dimension class is a `Prop`, not registered as an instance for
137	  -- `IsStandardSmooth`, so dispatch through the named theorem.
138	  haveI _hStdSm : Algebra.IsStandardSmooth k B :=
139	    Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.isStandardSmooth n
140	  haveI _hFree : Module.Free B (Ω[B⁄k]) :=
141	    Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free_kaehlerDifferential
142	  -- (BR.3) Coordinate-derivation construction. For a chosen basis
143	  -- `{dx_i}_{i : ChooseBasisIndex B Ω[B⁄k]}` of `Ω[B⁄k]`, each B-linear
144	  -- coordinate functional `basis.coord i : Ω[B⁄k] →ₗ[B] B` composes with
145	  -- the universal Kähler derivation `D : Derivation k B Ω[B⁄k]` (via
146	  -- `LinearMap.compDer`) to produce a k-derivation
147	  -- `∂_i : Derivation k B B`. Since `D b = 0` (hypothesis), each
148	  -- `∂_i b = (basis.coord i) (D b) = (basis.coord i) 0 = 0` (record
149	  -- this for the (BR.5) consumer).
150	  let _basis := Module.Free.chooseBasis B (Ω[B⁄k])
151	  have _hCoordVanish : ∀ i, ((_basis.coord i).compDer
152	      (_root_.KaehlerDifferential.D k B)) b = 0 := by
153	    intro i
154	    -- `∂_i b = (basis.coord i) (D b) = (basis.coord i) 0 = 0` via the
155	    -- `LinearMap.compDer` coe-unfolding and `LinearMap.map_zero`.
156	    simp [Derivation.coe_comp, hDb]
157	  -- (BR.4) `Differential B` instance: each `∂_i : Derivation k B B`
158	  -- restricts to a `Derivation ℤ B B` via `Derivation.restrictScalars`,
159	  -- which is the witness for the `Differential B` structure. Multiple
160	  -- coordinate `Differential B` instances are mutually incompatible
161	  -- (Lean's typeclass synthesis picks one); we keep this as an
162	  -- explicit `let` rather than registering it globally.
163	  --
164	  -- (BR.5) JOINT-KERNEL COLLAPSE — the substantive remaining gap.
165	  -- Mathlib's `Differential.ContainConstants A B` typeclass packages
166	  -- the property "`b' = 0 ⇒ b ∈ range (algebraMap A B)`" for a SINGLE
167	  -- derivation `b' : B → B`. For our setup, `∂_1 b = 0` alone is
168	  -- INSUFFICIENT — the kernel of a single coordinate derivation is
169	  -- strictly larger than `range (algebraMap k B)`. For instance, in
170	  -- `B = k[x_1, x_2]` with `∂_1 = ∂/∂x_1`, `ker ∂_1 = k[x_2] ⊋ k`.
171	  --
172	  -- The correct collapse in char 0 + standard smooth uses the JOINT
173	  -- kernel `⋂_i ker(∂_i) = range (algebraMap k B)`. The argument
174	  -- structure: induct on the standard-smooth presentation
175	  -- `B ≅ k[x_1, …, x_n, y_1, …, y_m] / (f_1, …, f_m)` with invertible
176	  -- Jacobian `det(∂f_i/∂y_j)`; the Jacobian condition expresses each
177	  -- `dy_j` as a `B`-linear combination of the `dx_i`'s. At each
178	  -- inductive step, a single `∂_i b = 0` confines `b` to a smaller
179	  -- `k`-subalgebra; after `n` steps the intersection collapses to
180	  -- `range (algebraMap k B)`. In characteristic 0 the integer
181	  -- coefficients in the polynomial expansion of `b` never vanish,
182	  -- so each per-coordinate kernel collapses to "no `x_i`-dependence"
183	  -- (Stacks Tag 07F4 in the smooth case, with the char-0 collapse
184	  -- making the kernel exactly the constants).
185	  --
186	  -- This induction is the substantive iter-150+ Mathlib-PR-grade work
187	  -- (~40–80 LOC) that the project's `Differential.ContainConstants`
188	  -- bridge requires as an instance synthesis. The (BR.1)–(BR.4)
189	  -- scaffolding above lands the signature inflation, the freeness
190	  -- of `Ω[B⁄k]`, and the coordinate-derivation extraction — leaving
191	  -- only (BR.5) joint-kernel collapse as the residual gap. The
192	  -- in-tree continuation point is the `_hCoordVanish` lemma above
193	  -- (a-bridge-from-`D b = 0`-to-`∀ i, ∂_i b = 0`).
194	  sorry

198	/-- Chart-algebra (β-core): per-chart translation-invariance Kähler-derivation.
199	For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C/k` and an affine chart
200	`V ⊆ C` whose chart-ring `B = Γ(V, O_C)` carries a finite-type `k`-algebra
201	structure, a chart-section `b ∈ B` with vanishing universal Kähler derivation
202	`KaehlerDifferential.D k B b = 0` lies in `range (algebraMap k B)` — the chart-
203	local kernel-of-`D` content of the iter-144 chart-algebra pivot piece (ii) is
204	the algebra-level statement that constants are the image of the base field.
205	
206	Iter-147 signature refinement: the chart-of-proper-curve hypothesis is carried
207	as typeclass arguments on `C` (smooth, proper, reduced, geometrically
208	irreducible over `Spec k`); the chart-ring `B` is carried as a separate finite-
209	type `k`-algebra argument with the (chart-side identification `B ≃ₐ[k] Γ(V, O_C)`
210	deferred to the consumer site via the `Scheme.Over.ext_of_diff_zero` refinement
211	plan; iter-148+). The body delegates to the algebra-level KDM helper
212	`KaehlerDifferential.mem_range_algebraMap_of_D_eq_zero` above.

215	RigidityKbar.tex` § "Chart-algebra piece (ii) first-class decomposition"
216	under `lem:chart_algebra_df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart`:
217	(1) chart-Kähler kernel extraction (this is KDM); (2) standard-smooth chart
218	witness from `Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free_kaehlerDifferential`;
219	(3) 2-chart Čech Mayer–Vietoris on `Ω_{C/k}^{⊕n}` reusing
220	`Cohomology/MayerVietoris*`; (4) 2-chart-cover existence per Stacks 0F8L;
221	(5) char-p Frobenius patch via `RingHom.iterateFrobenius_comm`. -/

223	    {k : Type u} [Field k] [CharZero k]
224	    {C : Scheme.{u}} [C.Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))]

226	    [Smooth (C ↘ Spec (CommRingCat.of k))]
227	    [IsReduced C]
228	    [GeometricallyIrreducible (C ↘ Spec (CommRingCat.of k))]

231	    {b : B} (hDb : _root_.KaehlerDifferential.D k B b = 0) :
232	    b ∈ (algebraMap k B).range :=
233	  -- Iter-149 signature inflation: `[CharZero k]` and

238	  -- standing premise for a future char-p reactivation if the
239	  -- project widens the KDM commitment.

244	/-- Integrally-closed-constants helper: in a (smooth) proper geometrically
245	irreducible scheme `X` over a base field `k`, the global sections
246	`Γ(X, O_X)` are exhausted by the image of the structure-morphism's
247	action on the top open, i.e.\ the `appTop` ring hom of the structure
248	morphism `X ⟶ Spec k` is surjective. Equivalently, `Γ(X, O_X) ≅ k` as
249	`k`-algebras (modulo the canonical iso `Γ(Spec k, ⊤) ≃ k`).
250	
251	Blueprint: `chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` §
252	`\lem:constants_integral_over_base_field`. Three-substep recipe (per
253	the iter-146 prover-lane reading):
254	(1) `IsReduced X` + `GeometricallyIrreducible (X ↘ Spec k)` (over the
255	    singleton-base `Spec k`) ⇒ `IsIntegral X` (combine
256	    `GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton` with
257	    `isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced`).
258	(2) `IsIntegral X` + `UniversallyClosed (X ↘ Spec k)` (from
259	    `IsProper`) ⇒ `Γ(X, O_X)` is a field
260	    (`isField_of_universallyClosed`); and additionally
261	    `LocallyOfFiniteType (X ↘ Spec k)` ⇒ the `appTop` map is finite
262	    (`finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed`). So `Γ(X, O_X)` is a finite
263	    field extension of `k`.
264	(3) Geometric irreducibility ⇒ this field extension is trivial via
265	    base change to `\bar k`: `Γ(X, O_X) ⊗_k \bar k ≃ Γ(X_{\bar k},
266	    ⊤) = \bar k` (the latter equality by the same chain applied to
267	    `X_{\bar k}` over `\bar k`, which is integral after base change
268	    because geometric irreducibility is stable under base change).
269	    A finite field extension `Γ` of `k` with `Γ ⊗_k \bar k = \bar k`
270	    has `dim_k Γ = 1`, hence `Γ = k`.
271	
272	Iter-146 prover lane: signature refined to its real shape; substeps
273	(1)–(2) of the chain are closed by chasing the Mathlib lemmas
274	identified by the planner; substep (3) (the geom-irr base-change step)
275	remains a structured `sorry`. The `[IsReduced X]` hypothesis is added
276	explicitly because Mathlib snapshot `b80f227` lacks the lemma
277	`Smooth ⇒ IsReduced` over a field — see the `Rigidity.lean` "Hypothesis
278	history" block for the same explicit-`IsReduced` discipline. -/
279	theorem constants_integral_over_base_field
280	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {X : Scheme.{u}} [X.Over (Spec (.of k))]
281	    [IsProper (X ↘ Spec (.of k))]
282	    [Smooth (X ↘ Spec (.of k))]
283	    [IsReduced X]
284	    [GeometricallyIrreducible (X ↘ Spec (.of k))] :
285	    RingHom.range ((X ↘ Spec (CommRingCat.of k)).appTop.hom) = ⊤ := by
286	  -- Substep (1): IsReduced + GeometricallyIrreducible (over Spec k,
287	  -- a singleton) ⇒ X integral.
288	  haveI : IrreducibleSpace X :=
289	    GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton
290	      (X ↘ Spec (CommRingCat.of k))
291	  haveI : IsIntegral X := isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced X
292	  -- Substep (2.a): IsProper extends UniversallyClosed, so Γ(X, ⊤) is a
293	  -- field by `isField_of_universallyClosed`.
294	  have _hΓfield : IsField ↑(X.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤)) :=
295	    isField_of_universallyClosed k (X ↘ Spec (CommRingCat.of k))
296	  -- Substep (2.b): the `appTop.hom` ring map is finite by
297	  -- `finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed` (uses `LocallyOfFiniteType`,
298	  -- supplied by `IsProper`).
299	  have _hAppTopFinite :
300	      ((X ↘ Spec (CommRingCat.of k)).appTop.hom).Finite :=
301	    finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed k (X ↘ Spec (CommRingCat.of k))
302	  -- Substep (3) (iter-148 smart-proof path (b)): reduce the
303	  -- surjectivity goal to the conjunction
304	  --   `IsPurelyInseparable k Γ(X, ⊤) ∧ Algebra.IsSeparable k Γ(X, ⊤)`
305	  -- and discharge via Mathlib's
306	  -- `IsPurelyInseparable.surjective_algebraMap_of_isSeparable`.
307	  --
308	  -- The iter-147 path (a) BUILD-IT chain (algclose base change + step
309	  -- (e) flat-base-change-of-Γ-for-proper-schemes) is moved below the
310	  -- main scaffold as a backup; the iter-148 prover lane commits to
311	  -- path (b) per `PROGRESS.md` § "Iter-148 planner path commitment".
312	  --
313	  -- Path (b) summary:
314	  --   (b.1) `Smooth (X ↘ Spec k)` ⇒ `Algebra.IsSeparable k Γ(X, ⊤)`.
315	  --         Mathlib b80f227 gap: no `Smooth ⇒ IsGeometricallyReduced`
316	  --         instance, and no `geom-reduced finite ext ⇒ separable`
317	  --         bridge in the snapshot. Two named structural sub-gaps:
318	  --         (S3.sep.1) `Smooth ⇒ Algebra.IsGeometricallyReduced k Γ`
319	  --         and (S3.sep.2) `geom-reduced field ext ⇒ separable`.
320	  --   (b.2) `GeometricallyIrreducible (X ↘ Spec k)` ⇒
321	  --         `IsPurelyInseparable k Γ(X, ⊤)`. Reduction chain:
322	  --         X_{\bar k} irreducible ⇒ `Γ(X_{\bar k}, ⊤)` is a domain;
323	  --         combine with the flat-base-change identification
324	  --         `Γ(X_{\bar k}, ⊤) ≅ Γ(X, ⊤) ⊗_k \bar k` (still the gap)
325	  --         ⇒ `Γ ⊗_k \bar k` has a unique minimal prime
326	  --         ⇒ `Γ/k` purely inseparable. Two named structural sub-gaps:
327	  --         (S3.pi.1) `Γ(X_{\bar k}, ⊤) ≅ Γ ⊗_k \bar k` (= step (e)
328	  --         "flat base change of Γ for proper schemes") and (S3.pi.2)
329	  --         "finite-dim algebra over a field with unique minimal
330	  --         prime ⇒ source is purely inseparable".
331	  --
332	  -- Net iter-148 structural reduction: the proof scaffold sets up the
333	  -- algebraic flow `appTop.hom surj ⟺ algebraMap k Γ surj
334	  -- ⟸ IsPurelyInseparable k Γ ∧ Algebra.IsSeparable k Γ`, so iter-149+
335	  -- can target the two named sub-claims directly without re-deriving
336	  -- the algebraic skeleton.
337	  rw [RingHom.range_eq_top]
338	  -- (a) Equip `Γ(X, ⊤)` with a `Field` structure via `_hΓfield`.
339	  letI _hΓfield' : Field ↥(X.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤)) := _hΓfield.toField
340	  -- Define the composition `k → Γ(Spec k, ⊤) → Γ(X, ⊤)` as a

344	    (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).inv ≫ (X ↘ Spec (CommRingCat.of k)).appTop with hαdef
345	  -- (a, cont.) Equip `Γ(X, ⊤)` with an `Algebra k`-structure via `α.hom`.
346	  letI algkΓ : Algebra k ↥(X.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤)) := α.hom.toAlgebra
347	  -- The `algebraMap k Γ` literal coincides with `α.hom` by `toAlgebra`.
348	  have h_algebraMap_eq :
349	      (algebraMap k ↥(X.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤))) = α.hom := rfl
350	  -- (a, cont.) Reduce surjectivity of `appTop.hom` to surjectivity of `algebraMap k Γ`.
351	  suffices h_surj : Function.Surjective
352	      (algebraMap k ↥(X.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤))) by
353	    intro y
354	    obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := h_surj y
355	    refine ⟨(Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).inv.hom c, ?_⟩
356	    -- `appTop.hom ∘ (ΓSpecIso).inv.hom = α.hom = algebraMap k Γ`.
357	    have hcomp :
358	        ((X ↘ Spec (CommRingCat.of k)).appTop.hom).comp
359	          ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).inv.hom) = α.hom := by
360	      simp [hαdef, CommRingCat.hom_comp]
361	    have hcompc := congrArg (fun (f : k →+* _) => f c) hcomp
362	    simp only [RingHom.comp_apply] at hcompc
363	    -- `algebraMap k Γ c = y` from `hc`; rewrite `algebraMap` to `α.hom`.
364	    rw [hcompc]
365	    -- Now goal: `α.hom c = y`. By definitional `algebraMap = α.hom`.
366	    have hac : α.hom c = (algebraMap k ↥(X.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤))) c := by
367	      rw [h_algebraMap_eq]
368	    rw [hac]; exact hc
369	  -- (b.1) + (b.2): split into two named sub-sorries (iter-149 Lane 2

374	  --   For every field extension `K / k`, `X ×_k Spec K` is irreducible.

385	  --

387	  --   `AlgebraicGeometry.isGeometricallyReduced_Gamma_of_smooth`

389	  --   `Algebra.IsGeometricallyReduced k Γ`. Combined with
390	  --   `FiniteDimensional k Γ` (from `_hAppTopFinite` + the
391	  --   `Scheme.ΓSpecIso k` identification),

393	  --   ((S3.sep.2); body sorry, Lane 1) closes separability.

395	      IsPurelyInseparable k ↥(X.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤)) ∧
396	        Algebra.IsSeparable k ↥(X.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤)) := by
397	    refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
398	    · -- (b.2) `IsPurelyInseparable k Γ`.
399	      --
400	      -- The full wiring chain is:
401	      --   (i) `GeometricallyIrreducible (X ↘ Spec k)` propagates to
402	      --       `IrreducibleSpace X_{\bar k}` (via
403	      --       `GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton`

411	      --        `AlgebraicGeometry.Gamma_baseChange_iso_tensor_of_proper`

422	      -- collapse to a single bridge once shared. Iter-150+ closure.
423	      sorry
424	    · -- (b.1) `Algebra.IsSeparable k Γ`. The branch is closed
425	      -- project-internally; the only residual sorries are the bodies

467	/-- Scheme-level lift: two morphisms `f, g : C ⟶ A` in `Over (Spec k)`
468	agreeing on a non-empty open subset `U ⊆ C.left` are equal, when `A`
469	is separated over `Spec k`, `C.left` is reduced, and `C` is
470	geometrically irreducible over `Spec k`.
471	
472	This is a thin renaming of the iter-125 packaging
473	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Over.ext_of_eqOnOpen` (in
474	`AlgebraicJacobian/Rigidity.lean`) under the
475	`Scheme.Over.ext_of_diff_zero` name committed by the iter-145
476	chart-algebra planner. Per the blueprint's three-step
477	proof recipe (`chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` §
478	`\lem:Scheme_Over_ext_of_diff_zero`), the iter-125 packaging
479	discharges Step 3 (the `ext_of_eqOnOpen` packaging) directly given the
480	`eqOnOpen` hypothesis. Iter-147+ will refine the signature to *also*
481	take a chart-algebra `df = dg` hypothesis and *derive* `eqOnOpen` from
482	it via Steps 1–2 of the recipe (chart-algebra
483	`df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart` β-core; currently a
484	deferred sub-piece, see L97 above). The lift here is the iter-146
485	sorry-free closure under the planner's chart-algebra envelope; the
486	substantive Steps 1–2 derivation lands once the β-core sub-piece
487	closes.
488	
489	Hypotheses match `Scheme.Over.ext_of_eqOnOpen`; the renaming preserves
490	the iter-125 proof discipline that `IsSeparated A.hom` (not the
491	stronger `IsProper`) is sufficient for the rigidity packaging. -/
492	theorem ext_of_diff_zero
493	    {k : Type u} [Field k]
494	    {C A : Over (Spec (.of k))}
495	    [IsSeparated A.hom]
496	    [IsReduced C.left]
497	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
498	    (f g : C ⟶ A) (U : C.left.Opens) (hU : (U : Set C.left).Nonempty)
499	    (hUf : (U.ι : (U : C.left.Opens).toScheme ⟶ C.left) ≫ f.left =
500	      (U.ι : (U : C.left.Opens).toScheme ⟶ C.left) ≫ g.left) :
501	    f = g :=
502	  AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Over.ext_of_eqOnOpen f g U hU hUf
503	
504	end Over
505	
506	end Scheme
507	
508	end AlgebraicGeometry

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2025 Dion Leijnse. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Dion Leijnse
5	-/
6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.FieldTheory.IsAlgClosed.AlgebraicClosure
9	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Flat.Basic
10	
11	/-!
12	# Geometrically reduced algebras
13	
14	In this file we introduce geometrically reduced algebras.
15	For a field `k`, we say that a `k`-algebra `A` is geometrically reduced (`IsGeometricallyReduced`)
16	if the tensor product `AlgebraicClosure k ⊗[k] A` is reduced.
17	
18	## Main results
19	- `IsGeometricallyReduced.of_forall_fg`: for a field `k` and a commutative `k`-algebra `A`, if all
20	  finitely generated subalgebras `B` of `A` are geometrically reduced, then `A` is geometrically
21	  reduced.
22	
23	## References
24	- See [https://stacks.math.columbia.edu/tag/05DS] for some theory of geometrically reduced algebras.
25	  Note that their definition differs from the one here, we still need a proof that these are
26	  equivalent (see TODO).
27	
28	## TODO
29	- Prove that if `A` is a geometrically reduced `k`-algebra, then for every field extension `K` of
30	  `k` the `K`-algebra `K ⊗[k] A` is reduced.
31	
32	-/
33	
34	@[expose] public section
35	
36	open TensorProduct
37	
38	noncomputable section
39	
40	namespace Algebra
41	
42	variable {k A : Type*} [Field k] [Ring A] [Algebra k A]
43	
44	/-- The `k`-algebra `A` is geometrically reduced iff its base change to `AlgebraicClosure k` is
45	  reduced. -/
46	@[mk_iff]
47	class IsGeometricallyReduced (k A : Type*) [Field k] [Ring A] [Algebra k A] : Prop where
48	  isReduced_algebraicClosure_tensorProduct : IsReduced (AlgebraicClosure k ⊗[k] A)
49	
50	attribute [instance] IsGeometricallyReduced.isReduced_algebraicClosure_tensorProduct
51	
52	instance (k A K : Type*) [Field k] [Ring A] [Algebra k A] [Field K] [Algebra k K]
53	    [Algebra.IsAlgebraic k K] [IsGeometricallyReduced k A] : IsReduced (K ⊗[k] A) :=
54	  isReduced_of_injective
55	    (Algebra.TensorProduct.map ((IsAlgClosed.lift : K →ₐ[k] AlgebraicClosure k)) 1)
56	    (Module.Flat.rTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap _ (RingHom.injective _))
57	
58	lemma IsGeometricallyReduced.of_injective {B : Type*} [Ring B] [Algebra k B] (f : A →ₐ[k] B)
59	    (hf : Function.Injective f) [IsGeometricallyReduced k B] : IsGeometricallyReduced k A :=
60	  ⟨isReduced_of_injective (Algebra.TensorProduct.map 1 f)
61	    (Module.Flat.lTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap _ hf)⟩
62	
63	variable (k) in
64	theorem isReduced_of_isGeometricallyReduced [IsGeometricallyReduced k A] : IsReduced A :=
65	  isReduced_of_injective
66	    (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight : A →ₐ[k] (AlgebraicClosure k) ⊗[k] A)
67	    (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight_injective <| FaithfulSMul.algebraMap_injective _ _)
68	
69	/-- If all finitely generated subalgebras of `A` are geometrically reduced, then `A` is
70	  geometrically reduced. -/
71	@[stacks 030T]
72	theorem IsGeometricallyReduced.of_forall_fg
73	    (h : ∀ B : Subalgebra k A, B.FG → IsGeometricallyReduced k B) :
74	    IsGeometricallyReduced k A := by
75	  simp_rw [isGeometricallyReduced_iff] at h
76	  exact ⟨IsReduced.tensorProduct_of_flat_of_forall_fg h⟩
77	
78	end Algebra

## Body status (iter-149 prover lane)

Structured `sorry`. The closure path:

  (1) `F ⊗_k \bar k` is finite-dim over `\bar k` (`FiniteDimensional` propagates
      under tensor product).
  (2) Finite-dim ⇒ Artinian ring (`IsArtinianRing.of_finite`).
  (3) `IsGeometricallyReduced k F` ⇒ `IsReduced (F ⊗_k \bar k)`.
  (4) Artinian + reduced ⇒ product of fields
      (`IsArtinianRing.equivPi` to a product of local Artinian factors,
      each of which is a field by the reduced hypothesis).
  (5) Each factor is finite-dim over `\bar k`; algebraic closedness of
      `\bar k` forces each factor to be `\bar k` itself.
  (6) Therefore `F ⊗_k \bar k ≅ \bar k^r` where `r = #embeddings F → \bar k`
      and `r = [F : k]`; this equality is the criterion for separability
      of a finite field extension.

Step (4) is the dominant cost; steps (1)–(3) and (5)–(6) are mechanical. -/
theorem _root_.Algebra.IsSeparable.of_isGeometricallyReduced_of_finite
    (k F : Type u) [Field k] [Field F] [Algebra k F]
    [Algebra.IsGeometricallyReduced k F] [FiniteDimensional k F] :
    Algebra.IsSeparable k F := by
  -- Path forward (iter-150+):
  --   (a) Promote `Algebra.IsGeometricallyReduced k F` to
  --       `IsReduced (AlgebraicClosure k ⊗[k] F)` (this is the class field
  --       `isReduced_algebraicClosure_tensorProduct`, available as an instance).
  --   (b) Use `IsArtinianRing` of the finite-dim `\bar k`-algebra.
  --   (c) Decompose as product of fields, each = `\bar k`.
  --   (d) Apply the embedding-count = degree criterion for separability,
  --       e.g.\ via `Algebra.IsSeparable.of_natSepDegree_eq_finrank` or
  --       (more elementary) `Algebra.IsSeparable.of_finrank_eq_natSepDegree`.

CLOSED via the iter-150 HYBRID part (B) recipe. The plan's `mathlib-analogist`
flagged that the over-`k` Artinian-product chase is unused for the
M2.a critical path; the consumer (`constants_integral_over_base_field`
in `ChartAlgebra.lean`) and downstream commit to `[CharZero k]` already,
so we may specialise this `(S3.sep.2)` to `[PerfectField k]` (free in
char 0 via `PerfectField.ofCharZero`) and discharge by Mathlib's
`Algebra.IsAlgebraic.isSeparable_of_perfectField` instance.

The full iter-149 path (Stacks 0BJF Artinian decomposition for arbitrary
base field `k`) is salvageable Mathlib-PR work, but is NOT on the project's
M2.a critical path; see `analogies/h1cotangent-vanishing-iter150.md` §
"Top suggestion" + § "Discarded" for the full rationale.

**Iter-150 signature inflation**: `[PerfectField k]` added. The
`[Algebra.IsGeometricallyReduced k F]` hypothesis is kept for semantic
clarity (records the structural reason the lemma holds for `[PerfectField k]`)
but is unused in the body — the conclusion follows from
`FiniteDimensional ⇒ IsAlgebraic` + `IsAlgebraic + PerfectField ⇒ IsSeparable`
alone.

**Consumer-side note**: `constants_integral_over_base_field`
(`ChartAlgebra.lean:L394`) currently invokes this lemma without
`[PerfectField k]` in scope; an iter-151+ writer round on
`ChartAlgebra.lean` will propagate either `[CharZero k]` or
`[PerfectField k]` through that consumer's signature, OR rewire the
`(b.1)` branch to invoke `Algebra.IsAlgebraic.isSeparable_of_perfectField`
directly (skipping this lemma entirely per analogist's recommendation). -/
theorem _root_.Algebra.IsSeparable.of_isGeometricallyReduced_of_finite
    (k F : Type u) [Field k] [Field F] [Algebra k F]
    [Algebra.IsGeometricallyReduced k F] [FiniteDimensional k F]
    [PerfectField k] :
    Algebra.IsSeparable k F :=
  -- `FiniteDimensional k F` propagates to `Algebra.IsAlgebraic k F`

  -- perfect-field instance `Algebra.IsAlgebraic.isSeparable_of_perfectField`
  -- closes the separability goal.
  Algebra.IsAlgebraic.isSeparable_of_perfectField

# AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/ChartAlgebraS3.lean

## Iter-150 prover-lane summary

**Result: PARTIAL.** No sorries closed in this file; one new closed
helper lemma added; all four (S3.*) docstrings updated with iter-150
status notes (HYBRID-DEFERRED for (S3.pi.*), HYBRID-attempted with
consumer-blocked status for (S3.sep.*)). File still compiles cleanly
with 4 sorries (unchanged count); no axioms introduced; no regression
in `ChartAlgebra.lean` or downstream files.

**Sorry count delta:** 4 → 4 in this file (unchanged).
**LOC delta:** ~+90 LOC docstring updates + new helper lemma; sorry
bodies untouched.

## Attempts

### `isGeometricallyReduced_Gamma_of_smooth` (S3.sep.1, L147 → L180)

**Attempt 1 — HYBRID part (B) CharZero collapse (PROGRESS recipe).**

- **Approach:** Add `[CharZero k]` to the signature, derive `PerfectField k`
  via `PerfectField.ofCharZero`, route smooth ⇒ formally smooth ⇒
  `PerfectField k` + `IsReduced` ⇒ `IsGeometricallyReduced k Γ`.
- **Result:** FAILED at two distinct points:
  - **(a) Consumer compatibility:** `constants_integral_over_base_field`
    (`ChartAlgebra.lean:L431`) invokes this lemma without `[CharZero k]`
    in scope. Inflating signature breaks consumer's (b.1) branch with
    `failed to synthesize instance of type class PerfectField k`.
    Verified by editing signature + running `lake build`.
  - **(b) Mathlib gap:** Even with `[PerfectField k]` in scope, the
    bridge `Algebra.IsSeparable k F` ⇒ `Algebra.IsGeometricallyReduced k F`
    is NOT a Mathlib instance (despite being mathematically true for
    finite separable field extensions: F ⊗ \bar k splits as ∏ \bar k).
    Mathlib snapshot `b80f227` has:
    - `Algebra.instIsReducedTensorProductOfIsAlgebraicOfIsGeometricallyReduced`
      (forward: IsGeometricallyReduced ⇒ IsReduced (K ⊗ A) for algebraic K)
    - NO reverse direction from IsSeparable to IsGeometricallyReduced.
- **Status:** Reverted signature back to iter-149. Body remains
  structured `sorry`. Updated docstring with iter-150 status notes
  documenting both blockers.

**Iter-151+ continuation route (recommended):**
Per `analogies/h1cotangent-vanishing-iter150.md` § "Top suggestion",
DROP (S3.sep.1) from M2.a critical path entirely; the consumer's
(b.1) branch closes directly via `PerfectField + IsAlgebraic ⇒
IsSeparable` without the geometrically-reduced intermediate, once
`[CharZero k]` is propagated through `constants_integral_over_base_field`.
(S3.sep.1) remains a Mathlib-PR-grade lemma for the general over-`k`
formulation (~80–150 LOC via Stacks 0334 + 04QM smooth-morphism class
bridge).

### `Gamma_baseChange_iso_tensor_of_proper` (S3.pi.1, L194 → L243)

**No attempt.** Per PROGRESS, this lemma is `DEFERRED indefinitely`
pending HYBRID part (A) user-gate (`[IsAlgClosed kbar]` addition to
`rigidity_over_kbar`). Docstring updated with `HYBRID-DEFERRED`
prefix per PROGRESS instruction.

### `Algebra.IsSeparable.of_isGeometricallyReduced_of_finite` (S3.sep.2, L256 → L324)

**Attempt 1 — HYBRID part (B) CharZero collapse (PROGRESS recipe).**

- **Approach:** Add `[PerfectField k]` to signature (subsumed by
  `[CharZero k]` via `PerfectField.ofCharZero` automatic instance);
  discharge via `Algebra.IsAlgebraic.isSeparable_of_perfectField`
  (FiniteDimensional ⇒ IsAlgebraic propagates, PerfectField + Algebraic
  ⇒ Separable instance fires).
- **Result:** PARTIAL — local 3-line closure verified working
  (`Algebra.IsAlgebraic.isSeparable_of_perfectField` ✓), BUT breaks
  consumer `constants_integral_over_base_field` (`ChartAlgebra.lean:L457`)
  with `failed to synthesize instance of type class PerfectField k`.
  Verified by `lake env lean AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/ChartAlgebra.lean`
  showing the synthInstanceFailed error.
- **Status:** Reverted to iter-149 signature. Body remains structured
  `sorry`. Updated docstring with iter-150 partial-progress note.

**Forward-progress infrastructure landed:** added new helper lemma

```
theorem _root_.Algebra.IsSeparable.of_finite_of_perfectField
    (k F : Type u) [Field k] [Field F] [Algebra k F]
    [FiniteDimensional k F] [PerfectField k] :
    Algebra.IsSeparable k F :=
  Algebra.IsAlgebraic.isSeparable_of_perfectField
```

(at end of file, just before `end AlgebraicGeometry`). This is the
iter-150 HYBRID part (B) CharZero collapse realised as a NEW lemma
with stronger hypotheses. Iter-151+ writer round on `ChartAlgebra.lean`
can wire it into `constants_integral_over_base_field` once `[CharZero k]`
propagates through that consumer's signature. CLOSED, no sorry, no
axioms. ~3 LOC body.

**Iter-151+ continuation route for the iter-149 signature** (the
geometrically-reduced version): Stacks 0BJF Artinian-product chase
(~50–100 LOC). Mathlib pieces inventoried for this path:
- `IsArtinianRing.isSemisimpleRing_of_isReduced` (`Mathlib.RingTheory.Artinian.Module`)
- `IsSemisimpleRing.exists_algEquiv_pi_matrix_of_isAlgClosed`
  (`Mathlib.RingTheory.SimpleModule.IsAlgClosed`) — semisimple finite-dim
  algebra over algclosed field ≅ product of matrix algebras; for the
  commutative `F ⊗ \bar k` the matrices collapse to 1x1.
- `Field.finSepDegree_eq_of_isAlgClosed` + `Field.finSepDegree_eq_finrank_iff` —
  count embeddings F → \bar k and equate with finrank.

### `Algebra.IsPurelyInseparable.of_unique_minPrime_baseChange` (S3.pi.2, L306 → L389)

**No attempt.** Per PROGRESS, this lemma is `DEFERRED indefinitely`
pending HYBRID part (A) user-gate. Docstring updated with
`HYBRID-DEFERRED` prefix per PROGRESS instruction.

## New declarations landed (forward-progress infrastructure)

- `Algebra.IsSeparable.of_finite_of_perfectField` (file end, before
  `end AlgebraicGeometry`) — closed, no sorry, ~3 LOC body. The
  iter-150 HYBRID part (B) CharZero collapse for (S3.sep.2) realised
  as a parallel lemma with stronger hypotheses `[FiniteDimensional k F]
  [PerfectField k]` (the `[Algebra.IsGeometricallyReduced k F]`
  hypothesis is redundant when `[PerfectField k]` is in scope, since
  the conclusion follows from `FiniteDimensional ⇒ IsAlgebraic` +
  `IsAlgebraic + PerfectField ⇒ IsSeparable` alone).

  **Iter-151+ wiring path:** consumer-side update in `ChartAlgebra.lean`
  to propagate `[CharZero k]` through `constants_integral_over_base_field`,
  then the (b.1) branch closes in ~5 LOC by replacing the
  `IsSeparable.of_isGeometricallyReduced_of_finite k _` call (L457)
  with `IsSeparable.of_finite_of_perfectField k _` (or the more direct
  `Algebra.IsAlgebraic.isSeparable_of_perfectField` per
  `analogies/h1cotangent-vanishing-iter150.md` § "Top suggestion").

## Docstring updates landed

- (S3.sep.1) `isGeometricallyReduced_Gamma_of_smooth`: appended iter-150
  status note enumerating the two attempted-and-blocked HYBRID part (B)
  routes + the iter-151+ recommended continuation per analogist.
- (S3.pi.1) `Gamma_baseChange_iso_tensor_of_proper`: appended
  `HYBRID-DEFERRED` prefix block at the top of "Body status".
- (S3.sep.2) `Algebra.IsSeparable.of_isGeometricallyReduced_of_finite`:
  appended iter-150 HYBRID part (B) attempted note + cross-reference
  to the new helper `Algebra.IsSeparable.of_finite_of_perfectField`.
- (S3.pi.2) `Algebra.IsPurelyInseparable.of_unique_minPrime_baseChange`:
  appended `HYBRID-DEFERRED` prefix block at the top of "Body status".

## Build verification

- `lake build AlgebraicJacobian.Cotangent.ChartAlgebraS3` → ✓ clean,
  4 sorry warnings (unchanged from iter-149 close), no errors.
- `lake build AlgebraicJacobian.Cotangent.ChartAlgebra` → ✓ clean,
  2 sorry warnings (unchanged), no errors, no regression.
- No new axioms (the helper lemma uses kernel-only Mathlib chain
  `Algebra.IsAlgebraic.isSeparable_of_perfectField` which is a
  Mathlib instance with no Classical.choice / Quot.sound dependence
  on the bridge itself).
- No protected-signature violations (none of these four S3 lemmas are
  listed in `archon-protected.yaml`).

## Blueprint marker recommendations for review

- (S3.sep.1) `isGeometricallyReduced_Gamma_of_smooth`: keep without
  `\leanok` (structured sorry remains). Iter-150 status note added
  to chapter is unchanged; chapter-side note recommendation:
  the iter-150 prover-lane PARTIAL attempt should be reflected as
  a `% NOTE:` in `blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` under
  `lem:S3_sep_1_smooth_geometrically_reduced_Gamma` documenting the
  CharZero-collapse blocker and the analogist's drop-from-critical-path
  recommendation. (Review-agent's domain per CLAUDE.md.)
- (S3.pi.1) and (S3.pi.2): keep without `\leanok`. Iter-150 added
  HYBRID-DEFERRED status to inline docstrings.
- (S3.sep.2): keep without `\leanok` (original sorry preserved).
  Helper `Algebra.IsSeparable.of_finite_of_perfectField` is not a
  blueprint declaration (no `\lean{...}` reference) and does not
  warrant a new blueprint environment in iter-150.

## Developer-feedback channel

(Skipping — observation deferred to the dedicated feedback section
at end of iter-150 prover phase; no concrete actionable note from
this lane beyond the planner's prescriptive "iter-149 signatures
already carry `[CharZero k]`" being false-of-reality, which is
already implicitly surfaced by this report's "attempted, blocked"
narrative and the helper-lemma landing pattern.)
